heading: Determining status of novel derivative products
label: 8306 Determining status of novel derivative products
notes: DefinitionFor definition of “including” as used in this section, see , Banks and Banking.section 5301 of Title 12

children.heading: Process for determining the status of a novel derivative product
children.label: a Process for determining the status of a novel derivative product

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Any person filing a proposal to list or trade a novel derivative product that may have elements of both securities and contracts of sale of a commodity for future delivery (or options on such contracts or options on commodities) may concurrently provide notice and furnish a copy of such filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission and the Commodity Futures Trading Commission. Any such notice shall state that notice has been made with both Commissions.

children.heading: Notification
children.label: B Notification
children.text: If no concurrent notice is made pursuant to subparagraph (A), within 5 business days after determining that a proposal that seeks to list or trade a novel derivative product may have elements of both securities and contracts of sale of a commodity for future delivery (or options on such contracts or options on commodities), the Securities and Exchange Commission or the Commodity Futures Trading Commission, as applicable, shall notify the other Commission and provide a copy of such filing to the other Commission.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: No later than 21 days after receipt of a notice under paragraph (1), or upon its own initiative if no such notice is received, the Commodity Futures Trading Commission may request that the Securities and Exchange Commission issue a determination as to whether a product is a security, as defined in .section 78c(a)(10) of this title

children.heading: Request
children.label: B Request
children.text: No later than 21 days after receipt of a notice under paragraph (1), or upon its own initiative if no such notice is received, the Securities and Exchange Commission may request that the Commodity Futures Trading Commission issue a determination as to whether a product is a contract of sale of a commodity for future delivery, an option on such a contract, or an option on a commodity subject to the Commodity Futures Trading Commission’s exclusive jurisdiction under .section 2(a)(1)(A) of title 7

children.heading: Requirement relating to request
children.label: C Requirement relating to request
children.text: A request under subparagraph (A) or (B) shall be made by submitting such request, in writing, to the Securities and Exchange Commission or the Commodity Futures Trading Commission, as applicable.

children.label: i
children.text: the Commodity Futures Trading Commission from requesting that the Securities and Exchange Commission grant an exemption pursuant to with respect to a product that is the subject of a filing under paragraph (1); orsection 78mm(a)(1) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: the Securities and Exchange Commission from requesting that the Commodity Futures Trading Commission grant an exemption pursuant to with respect to a product that is the subject of a filing under paragraph (1),section 6(c)(1) of title 7

children.heading: Withdrawal of request
children.label: E Withdrawal of request
children.text: A request under subparagraph (A) or (B) may be withdrawn by the Commission making the request at any time prior to a determination being made pursuant to paragraph (3) for any reason by providing written notice to the head of the other Commission.

children.label: A
children.text: under subparagraph (A) or (B) of paragraph (2), unless such request has been withdrawn pursuant to paragraph (2)(E), the Securities and Exchange Commission or the Commodity Futures Trading Commission, as applicable, shall, by order, issue the determination requested in subparagraph (A) or (B) of paragraph (2), as applicable, and the reasons therefor; or

children.label: B
children.text: under paragraph (2)(D), unless such request has been withdrawn, the Securities and Exchange Commission or the Commodity Futures Trading Commission, as applicable, shall grant an exemption or provide reasons for not granting such exemption, provided that any decision by the Securities and Exchange Commission not to grant such exemption shall not be reviewable under .section 78y of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Commodity Futures Trading Commission or the Securities and Exchange Commission may petition the United States Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit for review of a final order of the other Commission issued pursuant to subsection (a)(3)(A), with respect to a novel derivative product that may have elements of both securities and contracts of sale of a commodity for future delivery (or options on such contracts or options on commodities) that it believes affects its statutory jurisdiction within 60 days after the date of entry of such order, a written petition requesting a review of the order. Any such proceeding shall be expedited by the Court of Appeals.

children.heading: Transmittal of petition and record
children.label: 2 Transmittal of petition and record
children.text: A copy of a petition described in paragraph (1) shall be transmitted not later than 1 business day after filing by the complaining Commission to the responding Commission. On receipt of the petition, the responding Commission shall file with the court a copy of the order under review and any documents referred to therein, and any other materials prescribed by the court.

children.heading: Standard of review
children.label: 3 Standard of review
children.text: The court, in considering a petition filed pursuant to paragraph (1), shall give no deference to, or presumption in favor of, the views of either Commission.

children.heading: Judicial stay
children.label: 4 Judicial stay
children.text: The filing of a petition by the complaining Commission pursuant to paragraph (1) shall operate as a stay of the order, until the date on which the determination of the court is final (including any appeal of the determination).
